formula_note: Status-based coding keeps the one explicitly identified off-duty soldier in the military numerator.
uncertainty_note: High classification sensitivity. If coding were based on activity/location at the time of death rather than formal status, this component would become zero and dr_v1_direct would not be publishable.
quality_note: Numerically stable but conceptually fragile because it depends on a single coding decision.

formula_note: Civilian direct deaths equal total deaths in Israel during the 12-day episode minus the one explicitly identified soldier death.
uncertainty_note: Low-to-moderate uncertainty; the aggregate fatality count is strongly corroborated and the only classification issue concerns the single off-duty soldier.

formula_note: Set to zero because the same source set classifies all 28 reported fatalities as either civilian or one off-duty soldier.
uncertainty_note: This is a classification-completeness judgment, not an independent count of otherwise-unknown status deaths.
quality_note: Acceptable because the source set explicitly partitions the 28 dead into civilian and one soldier.

formula_note: Military stock scenarios: 169,500 active personnel plus 20,000 / 40,000 / 60,000 called-up reservists, multiplied by 12/30.44 months.
uncertainty_note: Scenario-bounded rather than measured. It captures active-duty and part of the reserve call-up but omits police, border police, prison service, and some emergency personnel, so it is better read as an exposed military floor.
quality_note: Publishable scenario denominator, but not a headcount observed directly in a same-window roster.

formula_note: Civilian stock begins from 10.1 million statewide population, subtracts the military scenario stock, subtracts 100,000 / 50,000 / 0 temporary absences abroad, then multiplies by 12/30.44 months.
uncertainty_note: Reasonably defensible because the home-front emergency was nationwide, but still modeled. Temporary absences abroad were not observed as a daily stock series.
quality_note: Good scenario-bounded denominator for a countrywide bombardment episode.

formula_note: dr_v1_direct = (27 / civ_person_months) / (1 / mil_person_months), with low/best/high using aligned low/central/high person-month scenarios. Recomputed from component estimates under current DR validation rules.
uncertainty_note: Publishable only under status-based coding of the one off-duty soldier death. If that death is reclassified out of the military numerator, the denominator pair remains usable but dr_v1_direct should be left null because military direct deaths become zero.
